subject: Re: Spherical aberration in fs_motion_and_ctf
body:
Thanks for bringing this up Juha, we recently noticed the same thing and
were about to post here :-)

--
Ricardo Diogo Righetto


Em qui., 20 de fev. de 2025 às 16:03, Juha Huiskonen <
juha.huiskonen@gmail.com> escreveu:

> Hello,
>
> I have a question about spherical aberration (Cs) in Warp and CTF
> parameter estimation. At low resolution, it should be kept fixed (2.7 mm in
> my case). However, I noticed that values change during CTF parameter
> estimation.
>
> Following the tutorial, I have run these two commands:
>
> WarpTools fs_motion_and_ctf \
> --settings warp_frameseries.settings \
> --m_grid 1x1x3 \
> --c_grid 2x2x1 \
> --c_range_max 7 \
> --c_defocus_max 8 \
> --c_use_sum \
> --out_averages \
> --out_average_halves
>
> WarpTools ts_ctf \
> --settings warp_tiltseries.settings \
> --range_high 7 \
> --defocus_min 2 --defocus_max 8 --window 256
>
> When I examine the tilt series XML files, I can see that the Cs values in
> the <CTF> block differ a lot from 2.7 - the values range from -6.0 to 8.0.
>
> How can I stop Warp from refining the Cs?
>
> Cheers,
> Juha
>
> --

> .
>
